user_query:
I need a small, reproducible "account import" pack from the CSV at input/users.csv and a simple HTML conversion of input/announcement.md.

Please do all of the following and save outputs under output/:
1) Read input/users.csv (columns: name,email,password,created_at). Produce a pretty-printed JSON array at output/users_with_ids.json where each object includes exactly these fields:
   - id: a UUID v4
   - name
   - email
   - email_b64: Base64 of the email
   - password_plain: keep the plaintext password here for this mock dataset only (so we can validate)
   - password_sha256: SHA-256 hash of the salted password string "onboard2026:" + password_plain (hex lowercase)
   - fingerprint_md5: MD5 hash of the string "<name>|<email>" (hex lowercase)
   - fingerprint_sha512: SHA-512 hash of the same "<name>|<email>" string (hex lowercase)
   - created_at_iso: ISO 8601 (UTC) timestamp string normalized to include a Z suffix
   - created_at_unix: Unix seconds (integer) derived from created_at_iso

   The array should preserve the input row order. Use deterministic, standard algorithms for all hashes and encodings.

2) Create a minified version of the same data at output/users_with_ids.min.json (one-line JSON; functionally equivalent to the pretty version).

3) Convert the Markdown file at input/announcement.md to HTML and save it to output/announcement.html. Ensure the main H1 heading is preserved as an <h1> element in the HTML.

4) Write an operational README at output/README.md summarizing:
   - How many users were processed
   - The exact steps you performed (list the algorithms used by name, the UUID version, and the exact salt string used for the password hash)
   - A short sample showing one user's id and hashes with most characters masked (show only the first 8 characters for each hash and the first 8 characters of the UUID)

Notes:
- Use consistent field names and JSON formatting.
- Validate the JSON and ensure the minified file parses to the same structure as the pretty-printed one.
- Keep this self-contained and reproducible.
